sentence: Subjective:
HPI: Emily Hernandez, a 20-year-old female, presents for a yearly follow-up for asthma, allergic rhinitis, and food allergy.
She was switched from Symbicort to Wixela due to insurance changes in April 2020.
She uses Zyrtec in the spring, summer, and fall for seasonal allergy symptoms, which work well for her.
She has a known peanut allergy but tolerates almonds and cashews.
She has been trying to reduce her use of her inhaler as she prepares to join the military and needs to demonstrate control over her asthma.
She reports using Wixela 250/50 twice daily, with occasional use of her albuterol inhaler for exertional activities.
She has not had any urgent care or ER visits for breathing issues in the past year and has not been on prednisone for breathing issues.
Past medical history: Emily has a history of asthma, allergic rhinitis, and food allergy.
Review of systems: She reports no other symptoms.

Current medications: I prescribed Wixela 250/50 twice daily for asthma; Zyrtec as needed for seasonal allergy symptoms; Albuterol inhaler as needed for exertional activities; and Epinephrine auto-injector (EpiPen) for anaphylaxis.
Objective:
Vital signs: Not mentioned.
Physical exam: The patient's ears have a little bit of wax on both sides.
Her lungs sound terrific.
Test Results: Not mentioned.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: Emily Hernandez, a 20-year-old female with well-controlled asthma, allergic rhinitis, and food allergy.
Problem list: Asthma; Allergic rhinitis; Food allergy; Anaphylaxis risk; Unknown.
Plan:
I prescribed Alvesco 80 mcg, two puffs twice daily for asthma.
I will call in a refill for her Albuterol inhaler and EpiPen.
I instructed her to take the Alvesco as directed and her Albuterol as needed for exertional activities.
I also instructed her to continue taking Zyrtec as needed for seasonal allergy symptoms and to stay away from peanuts and tree nuts.
Follow-up: I will schedule a follow-up appointment in four months to repeat her breathing test and assess her asthma control.
